article_title: Bylaw 14830 — Works and Services Payment-In-Lieu Reserve Fund Bylaw 2026

minutes_briefs.title: Burnaby creates reserve fund for developer payments in lieu of building works
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby has passed Bylaw 14830, which creates a special reserve fund to hold money that developers pay instead of building certain public works (like roads, sewers, or parks) themselves. This bylaw sets up a dedicated account so these 'payment-in-lieu' funds can only be used for the specific infrastructure projects they were meant for, and it requires the city to track and report on the money. The bylaw does not change how much developers must pay or when they pay — it just changes how the city manages those funds once received.
minutes_briefs.action_required: No action needed from business owners — this bylaw affects how the City of Burnaby manages developer payments, not what developers must pay.
minutes_briefs.source_body: Burnaby Bylaw 14830: Works and Services Payment-In-Lieu Reserve Fund Bylaw 2026
